name: Mole Negro Oaxaqueno
recipeIngredient: 2 large Chickens (about 3 1/2 lbs each), cut into serving pieces, or 1 7-pound turkey
2 small Heads of garlic, scored around the middle
1/4 lb Chilhuacle black or guajillo chilies
6 Fresh thyme sprigs, leaves only, or 1/4 tsp dried oregano
3/4 cup Melted lard or safflower oil
1 Ripe plantain, skinned and cut into thick slices
3 Thick slices of dried French bread or semisweet roll
recipeInstructions:
Oaxacan Black Mole Serves 10 to 12
The Chicken:
1. Place the chicken in a saucepan with garlic, onions, and mint. Add water to cover and season with salt. Bring to a simmer, cover the pan, and continue simmering until the chicken is tender (about 35 minutes).
2. Strain the broth, reserving it.
3. Remove stems from dried chilies, if any, slit them open, remove seeds and veins, and soak for about 30 minutes in hot water. Drain and reserve seeds.
4. Toast the reserved chile seeds in a frying pan over high heat until blackened; let cool.
5. Add broiled tomatoes to the blender jar with water, cloves, allspice, thyme, marjoram, and oregano.
6. Heat lard in a small frying pan; fry sesame seeds until golden brown. Strain fat back into the pan and add seeds to the blender jar.
7. Fry ingredients (except chilies and chocolate) one at a time, straining between additions and blending thoroughly. Add water or broth as needed.
8. Cook blended mixture with 1/4 cup lard over medium heat for about 15 minutes.
9. Blend remaining chilies with soaking water; add to fried ingredients with chocolate. Cook an additional 5 minutes.
10. Stir in chicken broth, salt, and more broth if the sauce is too thick. Cook until just coating a wooden spoon (about 35 minutes).
11. Adjust seasoning with salt and serve.
The Art of Mexican Cooking From the collection of Jim Vorheis
